name: Potstickers
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup spelt flour or whole wheat flour
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the vital wheat gluten flour, spelt flour (or whole wheat flour), and cold water until a dense dough forms. Add more flour if needed to achieve a workable consistency.
2. Knead the dough for a few minutes without folding it over. Allow the dough to rest for a few minutes before cutting into 10 chunks.
3. Bring a large pot of broth (made with water, white wine, soy sauce, nutritional yeast, garlic, and ginger) to a boil. Add the gluten chunks and cook until they puff up. Let cool.
4. Prepare the filling by chopping carrots, dried shiitake mushrooms, scallions, salt, rice wine, cornstarch, and white pepper in a food processor. Mix with shredded cabbage.
5. Process about half of the cooled gluten to create a texture similar to cooked ground meat. Add this to the filling mixture.
6. Prepare the dough for skins by mixing unbleached flour and boiling water until soft. Knead 5 minutes, adding small amounts of flour as needed to achieve a workable consistency.
7. Roll out the dough into 4-inch rounds, place a dollop of filling in the center, fold, and seal. Place finished potstickers on a floured plate.
8. Steam the potstickers until the skins become translucent, using layers of cabbage leaves if desired.
9. Prepare the dipping sauce by mixing soy sauce, rice vinegar, chopped scallion, grated ginger, and sugar (if desired). Serve with the potstickers.
